rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE content, with type scopes for constants and equations, implementation scopes for assignments.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions use only visible identifiers and maintain type consistency.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponents in component type contracts; use visible ports or features.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses assume/guarantee labels, eq for equations, const for constants, and assign for output bindings.
Generated AGREE code must preserve AADL scope rules and avoid package-qualified identifiers not in the model.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes; only visible ports are allowed.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
AGREE annex placement in system implementations
